blocks.text: DTC P0A11: Voltage stabilizer (DCDC) start signal line failure

The DC-DC converter prevents the electrical units from being reset due to a drop in 12V battery voltage when the car is restarted from idling stop (when the starter is driven) in vehicles equipped with idling stop systems using 12V batteries. The DC-DC converter is connected to the DC-DC startup (CST signal) line for the PGM-FI ECU to send a request to start boost operation and to the LIN communication line for sending failure diagnosis results to the PGM-FI ECU, The DC-DC converter sends a signal to the PGM-FI ECU via the LIN communication line when the self-diagnostic circuit detects an abnormality in the electrical unit. When the DC-DC converter receives a boost request from the PGM-FI, it drives the internal boost circuit to boost the 12 V battery voltage and supply it to other electrical units. When the PGM-FI ECU sends a CST signal to the DC-DC converter, if the CST signal reception notification is not sent from the DC-DC converter for more than the specified time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
When the PGM-FI ECU cannot receive the receive notification signal sent from the DC-DC converter for 1.0 second or longer.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (CST line) between PGM-FI ECU and DC-DC converter

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory without the PGM-FI warning light being turned on.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
